title: Nowhere FO Dense Conjecture
statement_text: A hereditary class of structures is nowhere FO dense if and only if first-order model checking is fixed-parameter tractable on it, equivalently, if and only if the class is monadically NIP.
context_text: Gajarský et al. [19] conjectured a general correspondence between monadic NIP (model-theoretic tameness) and tractability of first-order model checking. The paper cites this conjecture in the introduction and notes that its main dichotomy result confirms it in the special case of tournaments.
paper_contribution: Confirms the conjecture for hereditary classes of tournaments: FO model checking is FPT if and only if the class is monadically NIP, which coincides with having bounded twin-width (Theorem 1.1).
notes: Exact statement of the conjecture is not reproduced verbatim in the available text; reconstructed from context. Attributed to reference [19] in the paper. PDF extraction may be incomplete.
paper_title: First Order Logic and Twin-Width in Tournaments and Dense Oriented Graphs

title: Delineation of Tournaments
statement_text: Are tournaments delineated, i.e., is every hereditary subclass of tournaments monadically NIP if and only if it has bounded twin-width?
context_text: The notion of a delineated class was introduced in reference [11] of the paper. The paper notes that Theorem 1.1 answers affirmatively the question of whether tournaments form a delineated class, as posed in [11].
paper_contribution: Proves that tournaments are delineated: every hereditary subclass of tournaments is monadically NIP if and only if it has bounded twin-width (Theorem 1.1, condition 5).
notes: The question is attributed to reference [11] whose authors and year are not identified in the available text. Statement reconstructed from the sentence 'answering a question posed in [11]'. PDF extraction is truncated.
paper_title: First Order Logic and Twin-Width in Tournaments and Dense Oriented Graphs
